name: Sprouted Rye Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon Anise seeds, crushed with a rolling pin
recipeInstructions:
Chewy and quite zesty, these flavorful Sprouted Rye Cakes are excellent with cold corned beef or pastrami.
Preheat the oven to 300°F. Drain the sprouted rye very well. Finely chop it in a food processor, blender, or by hand (about 1/3 cup at a time). Add the anise seeds and salt, then blend in the egg until fully incorporated.
Mix in enough flour to form a dough that holds together as a cohesive ball. The amount of flour needed will depend on how dry the sprouted grains were after draining. This dough is very sticky; keep your fingers well-floured for handling.
Using two spoons, divide the dough into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter. Place each ball on a lightly greased or parchment-lined baking sheet. Flatten the balls with the well-floured palm of your hand or the floured side of a glass.
Bake for 35 to 40 minutes, turning once after approximately 20 minutes, when the bottoms have browned. Yield: 30-40 cakes.
Variations: Try other sprouted grains such as wheat or oats.
